body.p1: P2P ports are for node-to-node traffic. Nodes use P2P to discover peers, relay blocks, relay transactions, and stay synced.
body.p2: If you want to help the mainnet with inbound peers, forward only 18761/tcp on your home router to your node.
body.p3: RPC is for wallet and node commands. It can control funds, query wallet details, stop the daemon, and interact with your node. RPC must stay private.
body.p4: Never forward RPC 18762 to the internet. Keep RPC bound to 127.0.0.1 unless you fully understand private network access control.
body.p5: Use firewall rules that match your system and hosting provider. Keep examples simple and review them before applying.
